channel.title: The Git Reporter
channel.description: A code-native newspaper of meaningful software change.

item.title: OpenClaw Lets Agents Wake After A Command Exits
item.description: OpenClaw added an on-exit cron schedule so a gateway-owned watcher can wake an agent after a watched process finishes.

item.title: API Discovery: API Impot Particulier Turns Tax Proof Into An Agent Check
item.description: France's API.gouv catalog exposes a DGFiP tax-data API where agents must treat authorization and proportionality as the product, not an afterthought.

item.title: Codex Gives Subagents A Delivery Receipt
item.description: A Codex commit adds structured send and receive logs for multi-agent communication, making delegation easier to trace without making it today's lead.

item.title: Mario Zechner Makes Pi Sessions Render Their Own Evidence
item.description: Pi's latest extension change lets durable session entries carry custom rendering, turning a maintainer's minimal-core stance into visible public work.

item.title: Hermes Makes Agent Approvals Thread-Local
item.description: Hermes replaced a shared approval flag with thread-local state, then paired that control with secret redaction and delegation-budget tests.

item.title: API Discovery: API Entreprise Makes Company Checks A Permissioned Agent Step
item.description: API Entreprise gives agents a public map for company checks, but the source also makes the permission boundary impossible to miss.

item.title: Codex Reset The Meter After Background Work Became User Work
item.description: OpenAI's public Codex usage-reset note reads like a customer-relief post. The public Codex repository shows something more specific: a cleanup across auto-review prompts, subagent delegation, review-time suggestions, and reset-credit visibility, while the usage backend remains outside the public diff.

item.title: OpenCode's Hardening Day Removes Layer Shortcuts
item.description: Agent Pulse saw OpenCode as a July 1 hardening signal, and the source receipts point to layer cleanup rather than launch noise.

item.title: Tau Lets Outside Contributors Shape The Exit Door
item.description: Tau's smallest command change became a public contributor story when an outside branch added /exit and the maintainer followed with contributing rules.

item.title: OpenClaw Narrows Who Can Wake An Agent
item.description: A cluster of OpenClaw commits put scheduled wakes, ACP runtime controls, and browser proxy invocation behind narrower target and scope checks.

item.title: API Discovery: API Geo Turns French Places Into Agent Inputs
item.description: The api.gouv.fr catalog records API Geo as an open public geography API with a technical definition, rate limit, WGS-84 coordinates, and GeoJSON support.

item.title: Crush Stops Writing To Vanished Shell Sessions
item.description: A public Crush patch moved shell-message persistence behind a session-existence check, turning a disappearing workspace edge case into tested agent reliability work.

item.title: Mario Zechner Kept Pi Compaction Visible
item.description: Two Pi coding-agent commits repaired the visible status and regression-test trail around interactive compaction, keeping context management inspectable instead of silent.

item.title: Tau Turns Agent Architecture Into Onboarding
item.description: A Tau documentation rewrite moved the coding-agent project from build-log internals toward a public quickstart, reference, package, and license surface.

item.title: Ben Moved OpenWork Setup Toward Claim Links
item.description: A public start-guide patch shifted OpenWork's agent-first onboarding away from email signup and toward provisional workspace claim links.

item.title: Codex Moves Skill Instructions Into Model Metadata
item.description: A compact Codex patch makes skills usage instructions depend on model metadata instead of hardcoded model-name matching.

item.title: Ope Oginni Made OpenCode's Served TUI Carry Its Keys
item.description: A contributor patch moved OpenCode's external served TUI from implicit local assumptions toward explicit ServerAuth headers in its transport configuration.

item.title: Codex Makes Plugin Trust Runtime Policy
item.description: A new Codex plugin patch turns marketplace source restrictions into runtime filtering, loading, and cache-refresh behavior.

item.title: Dale Nguyen Kept Hermes From Poisoning Python Projects
item.description: A Hermes Agent contributor patch strips active virtualenv markers before terminal subprocesses can misdirect another project's Python tools.

item.title: Agent Safety Gates Catch Edge Cases
item.description: Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, and Hermes all turned ordinary boundary mistakes into explicit control failures.

item.title: Kshitij Kept Hermes Approvals In Their Thread
item.description: A public Hermes Agent patch by kshitijk4poor removed a process-global session key so command approvals stay attached to the originating session.

item.title: Mistral Vibe Makes ACP Compatibility A Release Contract
item.description: Mistral Vibe 2.18.0 turns ACP behavior, editor setup, auth cleanup, and fork-session limits into release-visible evidence.

item.title: Kieran Klukas Made Crush Share Its Config Safely
item.description: Three public Crush commits by Kieran Klukas turned shared config, OAuth refresh, and malformed tool-call state into tested failure boundaries.

item.title: OpenWork Puts Self-Hosting On The Checklist
item.description: OpenWork's June 24 code changes make deployment configuration, readiness, packaging, and user-flow proof visible as project maturity work.

item.title: Fenglanhua Made OpenClaw Prove Its Memory Migration
item.description: A public bug report turned an OpenClaw memory-store regression into a doctor-owned migration with tests, vector-row restoration, and explicit proof limits.

item.title: Model Catalogs Get Plain-English Receipts
item.description: LangChain and Pi are making model/provider changes easier to inspect before they become hidden assumptions inside agent runtimes.

item.title: Shoubhit Dash Made OpenCode Read MCP Resources
item.description: Two OpenCode commits by Shoubhit Dash turned MCP resources from connected-server metadata into tools an agent can list, template, and read with limits.

item.title: Agent Control Hints Become Runtime Contracts
item.description: Fresh OpenClaw, Codex, and Hermes changes show agent projects turning speed modes, chat metadata, and worker instructions into explicit runtime surfaces.

item.title: Google Antigravity After the Transition
item.description: The public record after the June 4 transition points to Antigravity as the packaged migration and operator surface, while Gemini CLI still carries open implementation repair.

item.title: Agent Tools Get Back-Pressure Surfaces
item.description: Fresh Gemini CLI and OpenWork patches show agent products turning hangs, publish failures, voice quotas, and provider outages into explicit runtime behavior.
